project.name: taugo
project.description: TauGo is an executable Go kernel for the τ operator (chap. III.8 of the InteroperabiliteAgentique monograph), exposing a single public API, Kernel.Decide(ctx, Exchange) → Decision, that selects a call regime (Deterministe | Probabiliste | Refus) at the agentic frontier. It is a pure-local, standard-library-only project built on strict 4-layer Clean Architecture (CI/CD removed in v0.1.2, ADR-0010). Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: PRD.md
nodes.summary: Spécification canonique (V0.3) du kernel τ organisée en cinq blocs : cadrage stratégique et anti-objectifs, théorie opérationnalisée (trois dimensions, cinq invariants I1-I5), architecture et modèle de données, pseudo-algorithme du dispatcher en 8 étapes, discipline d'exécution et roadmap.

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.name: drift.md

nodes.name: 2026-05-29-AUDIT-complet.md
nodes.summary: Rapport d'audit complet du kernel τ : verdict global, décompte par sévérité, 57 findings (majeurs, mineurs, informatifs) sur la conformité des invariants, l'étanchéité des couches, le dispatcher et la calibration, avec correctifs commités et faux positifs écartés par vérification adversariale.

nodes.name: i3_authority_asymmetry.go
nodes.summary: Encode l'invariant I3 (asymétrie de D-AUTORITÉ, fait institutionnel — Searle 1995) : détecte le bypass du verrou ontologique sans attestation au-delà du seuil de blocage et la garde de péremption du profil (anti-patron #3).
nodes.languageNotes: I3PerimptionLimite exposé en getter (anti-patron #7 : pas de global mutable dans tau/*) ; horloge injectable via EvaluateI3WithClock pour des tests temporels déterministes.

nodes.name: i5_composition.go
nodes.summary: Encode l'invariant I5 (composition conjonctive) : agrège les angles morts d'une pile agentique (union déduplicée triée) et vérifie la double borne max(|A_i|) <= M(π) <= Σ|A_i| ; BoundsHold est la cible de FuzzI5_CompositionConjonctive.
nodes.languageNotes: Types Pile/AngleMort comme alias de slices ; BoundsHold co-calcule bornes et union en une seule passe pour éviter un second parcours.

nodes.name: IsProfileExpired
nodes.summary: Indique si la date de révision de la décision est dépassée par rapport à l'horloge fournie ; une date de révision nulle est traitée comme jamais périmée. Brique testable de la garde anti-patron #3.

nodes.name: EvaluateI3WithClock
nodes.summary: Rend le verdict I3 avec horloge injectable : détecte le bypass de la garde de péremption puis, sur régime Probabiliste sans attestation, le franchissement du seuil AuthBlock via le score D-AUTORITÉ ventilé (ADR-0008) avec repli sur le proxy TauScore.

nodes.name: EvaluateI3
nodes.summary: Point d'entrée public de l'évaluation I3 : délègue à EvaluateI3WithClock en utilisant time.Now() comme horloge de référence.

nodes.name: Aggregate
nodes.summary: Calcule M(π), l'union des angles morts à travers les couches d'une pile, en sortie déterministe (déduplication puis tri lexicographique).

nodes.name: BoundsHold
nodes.summary: Vérifie que l'agrégation respecte les deux bornes de l'invariant I5 — max(|A_i|) <= |M(π)| <= Σ|A_i| — en une seule passe co-calculant bornes et union ; cible de FuzzI5_CompositionConjonctive.

nodes.name: EvaluateI5
nodes.summary: Rend le verdict I5 dans le pipeline du dispatcher ; renvoie Held par construction en V1 car la pile de couches n'est pas encore réifiée dans la Trace, BoundsHold étant fuzzé indépendamment.

nodes.name: drift.go

nodes.summary: Builds a single scored CorpusEntry by computing the three τ dimension scores for an Exchange and labelling its decision regime.

nodes.name: labelForDecision
nodes.summary: Maps a Decision plus dimension scores and attestation state to a canonical labelled regime string for the corpus entry.

nodes.name: Generate
nodes.summary: Generates the baseline (unannotated) balanced corpus of synthetic Exchange records as JSONL.

nodes.name: buildPlan
nodes.summary: Computes the per-branch builder plan, distributing n entries across the six τ regime builders according to branch weights.

nodes.name: buildRefusFrontiere
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic Exchange engineered to fall outside the τ frontier, producing a frontier-refusal case.

nodes.name: buildRefusI3
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic Exchange triggering the I3 ontological authority lock (high D-AUTHORITY without attestation).

nodes.name: buildRefusI4
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic Exchange triggering the I4 coherence-violation refusal (low D-SENS with high D-INVARIANT).

nodes.name: buildDeterministe
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic Exchange expected to resolve into the deterministic call regime.

nodes.name: buildProbabiliste
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic Exchange expected to resolve into the probabilistic call regime.

nodes.name: buildHysteresis
nodes.summary: Constructs a synthetic Exchange positioned in the hysteresis band between regimes.

nodes.name: weightsFor
nodes.summary: Returns the branch weight distribution for a named distribution profile, erroring on an unknown profile.

nodes.name: Generator
nodes.summary: Seeded corpus generator holding a deterministic PCG RNG; exposes Generate, GenerateAnnotated, and GenerateScored plus per-regime builders.

nodes.name: Annotator
nodes.summary: Narrow interface for the dispatcher dependency used to annotate corpus entries with their decided regime.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the generate-corpus tool; parses flags (count, seed, profile, output, annotate, scored) and dispatches to the appropriate Generator method.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Parses CLI flags, validates the distribution and count, opens the output sink, and drives scored/annotated/baseline corpus generation; returns a process exit code.

nodes.name: main_test.go
nodes.summary: Test suite for the generate-corpus tool covering byte-reproducibility, frozen golden hashes, distribution balance, annotation correctness, and CLI exit-code behavior.

nodes.name: calibrate.go
nodes.summary: Implements the `tau calibrate` CLI subcommand: parses flags, loads and validates the corpus via calibration.LoadCorpus, runs calibration, and writes the canonical profile, mapping corpus errors to exit codes.

nodes.name: runCalibrate
nodes.summary: Parses calibrate flags, loads and validates the corpus, fingerprints inputs, runs calibration.Calibrate, and writes the canonical profile to disk; returns an exit code.

nodes.name: corpusErrExitCode
nodes.summary: Classifies a corpus-loading error into the appropriate CLI exit code via typed-error matching (CalibrationError, validation, not-found).

nodes.name: calibrate_test.go
nodes.summary: Test suite for the `tau calibrate` subcommand verifying flag validation, exit codes, corpus loading errors, legacy schema migration, and date-revision parsing.

nodes.name: calibrate.go
nodes.summary: Core calibration logic: defines CorpusEntry with schema migration and validation, loads JSONL corpora, simulates regime agreement to tune thresholds, and produces a byte-canonical serialized profile.
nodes.languageNotes: MarshalCanonical re-serializes with sorted keys for byte-reproducible profiles; CorpusEntry.MarshalJSON hand-builds JSON to control field ordering.

nodes.name: Validate
nodes.summary: Validates a CorpusEntry: checks score ranges and the presence of a labelled regime, returning a typed validation error.

nodes.name: LoadCorpus
nodes.summary: Loads a JSONL corpus file, decoding each line into a CorpusEntry, migrating legacy fields and validating each entry.

nodes.name: Calibrate
nodes.summary: Tunes the atomic thresholds against a labelled corpus by counting regime agreement, then derives calibrated dimension weights into the output profile.

nodes.name: MarshalCanonical
nodes.summary: Serializes a profile to byte-canonical JSON with recursively sorted object keys, ensuring reproducible profile fingerprints.

nodes.name: sortedAny
nodes.summary: Recursively normalizes a decoded JSON value so map keys are emitted in sorted order, supporting canonical serialization.

nodes.name: CorpusEntry.MarshalJSON
nodes.summary: Custom JSON marshaller for CorpusEntry that hand-builds the object to enforce a stable field ordering for reproducible corpora.

nodes.name: CorpusEntry
nodes.summary: Labelled calibration corpus record carrying the three τ dimension scores, attestation/human-in-loop flags, and the expected/labelled regime; supports legacy-schema migration and validation.

nodes.name: thresholds_atomic.go
nodes.summary: Lock-free atomic threshold store for the six τ decision thresholds, encoding float64 values as millis in atomic ints for concurrent-safe reads and tuning.
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes float64 thresholds as int64 millis in sync/atomic values to allow lock-free concurrent access without mutable globals.

nodes.name: AtomicThresholds
nodes.summary: Concurrent-safe holder of the six τ decision thresholds with atomic accessors (Deterministe, Probabiliste, AuthBlock, SensCoherence, InvCoherence, HysteresisGap), Snapshot, and SetTuning.

nodes.name: NewAtomicThresholds
nodes.summary: Constructs an AtomicThresholds from a Thresholds value, panicking if the input is nil (internal invariant).

nodes.name: errors.go
nodes.summary: Typed error layer defining DispatchError, RefusError, and CalibrationError plus the diagnostic sentinels (ErrFrontiereFranchie, ErrPeremptionProfile, ErrIncoherenceI4, ErrVerrouOntologique) used across the kernel.
nodes.languageNotes: RefusError.Is enables errors.Is matching against diagnostic sentinels; DispatchError and CalibrationError implement Unwrap for cause chaining.

nodes.name: DispatchError
nodes.summary: Wraps a dispatch-stage failure with stage, exchange ID, and detail, exposing Error and Unwrap for cause propagation.

nodes.name: RefusError
nodes.summary: Represents a τ refusal carrying a stage and diagnostic; its Is method matches the canonical diagnostic sentinels for errors.Is.

nodes.name: CalibrationError
nodes.summary: Wraps a calibration failure with the profile version and underlying cause, supporting Error and Unwrap.

nodes.name: errors_test.go
nodes.summary: Test suite verifying error-message formatting, Unwrap propagation, and errors.Is sentinel matching for the three typed error kinds.

nodes.name: agentmesh.go
nodes.summary: App-layer bridge wiring: converts AgentMeshKafka exchanges into neutral τ Exchange values and streams them through a context-cancellable fan pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: StreamAsTauExchanges uses a select loop with adapter error draining to guarantee clean goroutine exit on context cancellation.

nodes.name: ToTauExchange
nodes.summary: Maps an AgentMeshKafka exchange DTO onto the neutral τ Exchange type, translating discovery-mode strings.

nodes.name: StreamAsTauExchanges
nodes.summary: Streams adapter exchanges as τ Exchanges over output and error channels, draining adapter errors and exiting cleanly on context cancellation.

nodes.name: discoveryModeFromString
nodes.summary: Translates a discovery-mode string from the AgentMeshKafka DTO into the τ DiscoveryMode enum.

nodes.name: arch_test.go
nodes.summary: Architecture-enforcement test suite that walks the package AST to keep the Clean Architecture sealing rules intact, forbidding tau/* from importing orchestration or bridge and banning concrete LLM imports outside app/ and bridge/llm/.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses go/build, go/parser and go/ast to statically verify import constraints (anti-pattern #6 guard TestArchNoConcreteLLMInDomain).

nodes.name: TestArchitectureLayering
nodes.summary: Table-driven test asserting that each layer's forbidden import set is never satisfied, enforcing the directional sealing between tau, orchestration, bridge and calibration packages.

nodes.name: TestBridgeNoTauImport
nodes.summary: Walks the bridge package tree parsing each Go file to assert no direct import of internal/tau/* leaks across the bridge boundary.

nodes.name: TestArchNoConcreteLLMInDomain
nodes.summary: Guards anti-pattern #6 by AST-walking the tau and orchestration domain packages and failing if any import path contains a forbidden concrete LLM vendor substring.

nodes.name: decide_bench_test.go
nodes.summary: Micro-benchmark suite for the dispatcher Decide pipeline across deterministic, probabilistic and refusal exchange paths, reporting allocations per decision.

nodes.name: BenchmarkDecide
nodes.summary: Benchmarks Dispatcher.Decide over the three canonical regime exchanges, building a fresh dispatcher and exchange per sub-benchmark.

nodes.name: benchDecide
nodes.summary: Benchmark helper that asserts the expected regime once then drives the Decide hot loop with allocation reporting.

nodes.name: dimensions_bench_test.go
nodes.summary: Micro-benchmark suite measuring the scoring functions for the three orthogonal dimensions D-SENS, D-AUTHORITY and D-INVARIANT.

nodes.name: BenchmarkScoreDSens
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the D-SENS scoring path against a built exchange using the default sens weights.

nodes.name: BenchmarkScoreDAuthority
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the D-AUTHORITY scoring path against a built exchange using the default authority weights.

nodes.name: BenchmarkScoreDInvariant
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the D-INVARIANT scoring path against a built exchange using the default invariant weights.

nodes.name: i1_conservation.go
nodes.summary: Production implementation of invariant I1 (conservation): τ moves the fixation instant without altering the quantity, so the decision must preserve the exchange's content magnitude.
nodes.languageNotes: Property-function style: a scalar Conserve predicate consumed by the fuzz harness (FuzzI1_Conservation).

nodes.name: EvaluateI1
nodes.summary: Evaluates invariant I1 over an exchange and decision, returning whether conservation holds for the produced decision.

nodes.name: i2_irreductibility.go
nodes.summary: Production implementation of invariant I2 (irreducibility): computes the migrating residue and proves it is non-empty and not offline-rewireable, so removing it would push the exchange outside the τ frontier.
nodes.languageNotes: Residu/Recablage/EvaluateI2 model the refutation property fuzzed by FuzzI2_Irreductibilite.

nodes.name: Residu
nodes.summary: Builds the migrating residue set from an exchange by collecting the components that cannot be fixed before integration.

nodes.name: Recablage
nodes.summary: Models the attempted offline rewiring of an exchange with named residue components removed, used to test whether the residue is genuinely irreducible.

nodes.name: EvaluateI2
nodes.summary: Evaluates invariant I2 by requiring a non-empty residue and verifying the rewired exchange falls outside the frontier, confirming irreducibility.

nodes.name: i4_coherence_test.go
nodes.summary: Test suite for invariant I4 (constraint coherence): table-driven incoherence checks plus EvaluateI4 cases covering nominal, I4-refusal, missing-ventilated-scores and unrelated-refusal scenarios.

nodes.name: TestIsIncoherent_TableDriven
nodes.summary: Table-driven test exercising IsIncoherent across the sens/invariant threshold boundary cases that define the silent-rupture condition.

nodes.name: TestEvaluateI4_NoVentilatedScores_Held
nodes.summary: Verifies EvaluateI4 holds when the decision trace carries no ventilated dimension scores, guarding the fallback behaviour after the Trace ventilation refactor.

nodes.name: TestEvaluateI4_OtherRefusal_NotApplicable
nodes.summary: Asserts that EvaluateI4 remains held when the decision is a refusal for a reason other than I4 incoherence.

nodes.name: i5_composition_test.go
nodes.summary: Test and benchmark suite for invariant I5 (conjunctive composition): validates Aggregate's stable sorted blind-spot union with deduplication and the M(π) lower/upper bounds over composed stacks.

nodes.name: TestAggregate_MultipleLayers_StableSortedUnion
nodes.summary: Verifies Aggregate produces a stable, sorted union of blind spots across multiple composed layers.

nodes.name: TestM_LowerBoundMaxLayer
nodes.summary: Asserts the composed magnitude M(π) is bounded below by the maximum single-layer blind-spot count, via BoundsHold.

nodes.name: TestM_UpperBoundSum
nodes.summary: Asserts the composed magnitude M(π) is bounded above by the sum of per-layer counts, via BoundsHold.

nodes.name: makeLargePile
nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic large composed pile of layers and per-layer entries used to drive the I5 benchmarks.

nodes.name: BenchmarkI5_BoundsHold
nodes.summary: Benchmarks BoundsHold over a large pile, tracking the optimised allocation profile noted in v0.1.1.

nodes.name: calibration_determinism_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end test suite building the tau binary to assert byte-deterministic calibration output, a fixed golden-corpus profile hash, and that an expired profile triggers an ontological refusal.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds and shells out to the CLI binary via exec.Command and verifies reproducibility with sha256 hashing.

nodes.name: buildTauE2E
nodes.summary: Builds the tau CLI binary into a temp dir for the end-to-end calibration tests.

nodes.name: sha256HexFile
nodes.summary: Computes the hex SHA-256 digest of a file, used to assert byte-identical calibration profiles across runs.

nodes.name: runCalibrateCanonical
nodes.summary: Runs the tau calibrate subcommand against a corpus into a fixed output path under canonical flags.

nodes.name: TestCalibrationDeterministic
nodes.summary: Runs calibration twice on the golden corpus and asserts the two output profiles are byte-identical by SHA-256.

nodes.name: TestExpiredProfileRefuses
nodes.summary: Injects a profile whose revision date is in the past and asserts the dispatcher returns a peremption refusal, guarding anti-pattern #3.

nodes.name: golden-corpus.jsonl
nodes.summary: Golden calibration corpus of 170 CorpusEntry records with real ventilated scores and pinned SHA-256, feeding the deterministic calibration tests (ADR-0012).
nodes.languageNotes: JSONL CorpusEntry schema: probabiliste 90 / deterministe 50 / refus_authority 30; hash pinned for byte-identity.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand what TauGo is: the executable kernel of the τ operator (monograph chap. III.8). It lays out the doctrine and anti-goals (TauGo is a decision kernel, not a framework, orchestrator, or LLM wrapper), the single public API Kernel.Decide returning one of three regimes (Deterministe | Probabiliste | Refus), and the strict 4-layer Clean Architecture. PRD.md is the canonical V0.3 spec that the rest of this tour traces through actual code, and CLAUDE.md captures the operational rulebook including the 7 guarded anti-patterns.

tour.title: Binary Entry Point
tour.description: cmd/tau/main.go is where the tau binary boots. It dispatches the decide and calibrate subcommands and runs the JSON-in / JSON-out Decide flow by building a dispatcher through app.NewDispatcher. The runnable examples/quickstart shows the same path from the Go-embedding side, calling Decide on three typed cases and printing the regime plus the ventilated dimension scores. Together they answer 'how does the kernel get invoked?' before we look at what it does.

tour.title: The Public API: Kernel.Decide
tour.description: internal/tau/operator.go is the sole public entry point of the τ core. It declares the Kernel.Decide interface and the canonical types every other layer speaks in: Exchange (the input), Decision and Trace (the fully-instrumented output), Score, Regime, and DiscoveryMode. This is the contract the README promised — one function, three possible regimes. Any change here risks an API rupture, which is why it is flagged as a sensitive module.
tour.languageLesson: In Go, the public surface is an interface plus exported types in a single package. Capitalized identifiers (Kernel, Exchange, Decide) are exported; lowercase ones stay package-private. Keeping the whole contract in one file makes the 'API rupture' boundary explicit and reviewable.

tour.title: The Validity Frontier
tour.description: Building on the Exchange type from Step 3, internal/tau/frontier.go implements FrontierCheck — the first-rank guard that gates whether the kernel may even proceed. It encodes the four classic frontier conditions; Inside() returns true only when none of them are met, otherwise the exchange is out-of-frontier and the kernel must Refus. Bypassing this guard is forbidden anti-pattern #2. diagnostics.go declares the canonical Refus diagnostic constants (DiagFrontiereFranchie, DiagVerrouOntologique, ...) that the frontier and later steps reference.

tour.title: The Three Dimensions
tour.description: Once past the frontier, τ scores an Exchange along three orthogonal dimensions, each in [0,1]. D-SENS (dsens.go) measures where meaning is fixed, weighting four probes including a reasoner-intent probe that consults the injected LLM client. D-AUTORITÉ (dauthority.go) measures the reach of the delegation chain. D-INVARIANT (dinvariant.go) measures support for integration invariants. score.go aliases the shared Score type and the clamp01 helper that bounds every probe to [0,1].
tour.languageLesson: The score.go alias (type Score = tau.Score) exists to break an import cycle: dimensions must speak the core's Score type without importing back into a package that imports dimensions. Go forbids import cycles outright, so type aliases are an idiomatic way to share a type across a layered boundary (ADR-0008).

tour.title: The Five Invariants I1-I5
tour.description: The three dimensions feed the five refutable invariants of chap. III.8.5. evaluator.go aggregates them into a single Statuses struct and exposes EvaluateInvariants, which runs I1 (conservation) through I5 (conjunctive composition) over an Exchange/Decision pair. i3_authority_asymmetry.go is the most consequential: it encodes Searle's 1995 institutional-fact asymmetry, forcing an ontological Refus when authority is blocked without an institutional attestation — or when the calibration profile has expired (a theme picked up in Step 9).

tour.title: Refuting Invariants by Fuzzing
tour.description: The invariants are not asserted — they are continuously attacked. fuzz_targets_test.go defines native Go fuzz targets FuzzI1..FuzzI5 that throw randomized Exchanges at each invariant to try to refute its property (chap. III.8.7). This is how TauGo treats its theory as falsifiable: an invariant survives only as long as no counterexample is found. This step shows the empirical backbone the README's invariant-status table rests on.
tour.languageLesson: Go's built-in fuzzing (func FuzzXxx(f *testing.F)) is a first-class part of the standard testing toolchain since Go 1.18. The engine mutates a seed corpus and re-runs the property function millions of times, persisting any crashing input as a regression seed — turning an invariant into an executable, refutable claim.

tour.title: The 8-Step Dispatcher
tour.description: internal/orchestration/dispatcher.go is where everything composes: it implements Kernel.Decide as an 8-step pipeline whose ordering is deliberate (PRD §10). The steps run the frontier check (Step 4), the D-AUTORITÉ ontological lock, the profile-expiry guard, ventilated D-SENS/D-INVARIANT scoring (Step 5), weighted regime classification, and the I1-I5 invariant evaluation (Step 6) — always returning a fully traced Decision. This is the orchestration layer the Clean Architecture keeps strictly below the tau core.

tour.title: Calibration & Drift
tour.description: The dispatcher's thresholds and weights come from a calibration profile. profile.go defines the Profile and Weights structures and DefaultProfile — the checked-in default that pins thresholds, the monograph version, and a date_revision. drift.go guards that profile: it flags it as stale when CPU, LLM, or corpus fingerprints no longer match the environment, or when the date_revision has passed (anti-pattern #3, the I3 expiry path from Step 6). A stale profile is not silently tolerated; it forces a Refus.

tour.title: The Bridges: LLM & AgentMeshKafka
tour.description: TauGo never embeds a concrete LLM. client.go declares a narrow Client interface (Fingerprint + Interpret) that the D-SENS probe from Step 5 consumes, and stub.go provides a deterministic Stub used by default so tests stay reproducible. The AgentMeshKafka bridge (adapter.go defines neutral DTOs and the streaming interface; file_adapter.go streams JSONL fixtures as those DTOs) connects the kernel to empirical traffic without leaking infrastructure types into the core. This is how the architecture isolates non-determinism at the edges.
tour.languageLesson: Dependency inversion in Go: the core declares a small consumer-side interface (Client) and accepts any implementation. The concrete LLM is injected at the app layer, while a deterministic Stub satisfies the same interface in tests — keeping the tau core free of any vendor import (anti-pattern #6).

tour.title: Wiring It Together
tour.description: internal/app/app.go is the application lifecycle layer that the entry point from Step 2 actually calls. NewDispatcher wires the DefaultProfile from Step 9 and the selected LLM backend from Step 10 (the deterministic stub unless real is configured) into an orchestration dispatcher from Step 8. This is the one place where concrete dependencies are injected, satisfying the Clean Architecture rule that the inner tau core never reaches outward.

tour.title: Architecture Enforcement
tour.description: The whole tour's layering is not a convention you have to trust — it is mechanically enforced. arch_test.go walks the package AST to keep the Clean Architecture sealing rules intact: tau/* may not import orchestration or bridge, and concrete LLM imports are banned outside app/ and bridge/llm/. anti_patterns_test.go guards the doctrine itself: no exported predictive API, the I3 date_revision is respected, and unmodeled observations are always reported. These tests are the capstone that keeps every boundary you just learned from eroding.
tour.languageLesson: These are Go tests that import go/ast and go/parser to inspect the source tree itself rather than its runtime behavior — 'architecture tests'. A forbidden import makes the test suite fail like any other red test, turning Clean Architecture rules into CI-grade guarantees (here, local make test).
